libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, or system property is indicated. OSGi behavior can be covered with lightweight in-process Bundle/BundleContext stubs; do not require a real OSGi container unless local verification proves it necessary.

deterministic_setup.reason: jersey-core 1.14 declares this provided OSGi API and its OsgiRegistry references BundleReference, which is absent from the older org.osgi:osgi_R4_core:1.0 dependency.
jersey-core 1.14 declares JAXB as provided and core provider classes such as AbstractListElementProvider directly use javax.xml.bind types, which are not present in modern JDKs.
jersey-core 1.14 declares JavaMail as provided and includes MIME multipart entity provider code that directly references javax.mail types.
Jersey component lifecycle code dynamically looks for javax.annotation.PostConstruct, which is no longer supplied by modern JDKs.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8875 [Automation] java run fails for com.sun.jersey:jersey-core:1.14; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: If the old osgi_R4_core dependency remains on the test classpath alongside the newer OSGi API, duplicate org.osgi.framework classes may make classpath order relevant.
The JAXB API dependency is API-only; tests that create a real JAXBContext may need to avoid requiring an implementation unless local Gradle verification shows one is already available transitively.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jersey core 1.14 uses legacy provided Java EE and OSGi APIs; the current failure is specifically due to the old OSGi API artifact lacking org.osgi.framework.BundleReference.
